ideas.title: Create a minimalist, just-in-time training module
ideas.description: Develop and test a 5-minute micro-training integrated into the IIS login or workflow.
ideas.sourceTitle: Increasing use of immunization information systems for routine vaccinations in independent community pharmacies: A randomized controlled trial

ideas.title: Create a virtual fair network
ideas.description: Develop a scalable online platform for students to present projects and receive feedback, reducing geographic and resource barriers.
ideas.sourceTitle: Getting Everyone to the Fair: Supporting Teachers in Broadening Participation in Science and Engineering Fairs

ideas.title: Evaluating Workflow-Integrated Training and Protocol Support for Pharmacy Technician IIS Management
ideas.description: This study investigates whether coupling technician training with a structured workflow integration protocol sustains IIS data quality and pharmacy participation. Using a cluster randomized trial, it compares standard training against a combined intervention of training, a simplified data-entry checklist integrated into the dispensing workflow, and a feedback mechanism for technicians. Primary outcomes are the completeness, timeliness, and accuracy of IIS data submissions over a 12-month period, assessing resilience to real-world workflow pressures. The design directly tests if empowering technicians with clear protocols and feedback overcomes the displacement failure mode inherent in training-alone approaches.

ideas.title: Adaptive, Continuous Security Priming via Embedded Micro-Assessments
ideas.description: This redesigned system moves beyond a one-time, generic login module to a continuous, context-aware training and assessment layer woven into the user's daily workflow. It uses telemetry on user role, recent actions (e.g., file downloads, access requests), and performance on prior micro-challenges to deliver personalized, 60-90 second interactive scenarios at judicious moments. The system establishes a baseline and tracks competency progression over time, adapting prompt frequency and difficulty, thereby creating a closed-loop mechanism for reinforcing secure behaviors and addressing specific knowledge gaps just before they are most relevant.

ideas.title: Professional Identity Formation in Expanded Scope Training: A Double-Edged Sword for Healthcare Worker Retention
ideas.description: This research investigates the unintended consequences of training non-pharmacist providers (e.g., nurse practitioners) in pharmacy-based immunization systems (IIS). It challenges the assumption that such training, which inherently fosters a hybrid or expanded professional identity, is uniformly beneficial for retention. The study will examine whether the process of integrating new clinical roles and responsibilities within a retail pharmacy context creates identity conflict, perceived de-skilling of original roles, or performance pressure that may ultimately increase disillusionment and attrition, despite increasing technical competency. The goal is to develop training protocols that manage identity formation to support sustainable workforce expansion.

ideas.title: A one-time training intervention is sufficient
ideas.description: Test if ongoing support or booster training is needed to maintain IIS participation over time.
ideas.sourceTitle: Increasing use of immunization information systems for routine vaccinations in independent community pharmacies: A randomized controlled trial

ideas.title: Conduct a randomized controlled trial design
ideas.description: A future study should use an RCT to compare the MI intervention against a control group.
ideas.sourceTitle: Pharmacist-led motivational interviewing for diabetes medication adherence in a worksite wellness program.

ideas.title: Challenge shock as purely a negative phenomenon
ideas.description: Investigate if a temporary GPA dip could be a neutral or adaptive response to higher standards.
ideas.sourceTitle: STEMing the Shock: Examining Transfer Shock and Its Impact on STEM Major and Enrollment Persistence

ideas.title: A Comparative Implementation Science Study of Multi-State IIS Training Scale-Up
ideas.description: This research implements a standardized IIS training program across multiple states with diverse IIS platforms, using an implementation science framework like RE-AIM not just as an evaluation tool, but as an integrated, comparative study design. By prospectively applying the framework to plan, tailor, and measure the rollout in each distinct context, the study can isolate and analyze how platform-specific technical factors and state-specific organizational factors mediate outcomes like Reach, Adoption, and Implementation. This moves beyond simply scaling and evaluating a program to building a predictive model of implementation determinants for public health IT initiatives across heterogeneous environments, directly informing more effective future scale-up strategies for other health information systems.

ideas.title: Behavioral issues are a negative outcome.
ideas.description: Re-examine if reported behavioral issues are maladaptive or are expressions of frustration in an unchallenging environment.
ideas.sourceTitle: Spatially gifted, academically inconvenienced: Spatially talented students experience less academic engagement and more behavioural issues than other talented students.

ideas.title: Assessing Intervention Resilience Using Ecological Succession Models
ideas.description: This idea adapts a solution from ecology, where scientists assess ecosystem recovery after major disturbances like fires. The analogous solution involves comparing community structure and function before and after the disturbance using succession models to identify resilient components and altered trajectories. Adapted to the research context, we would replicate a pre-pandemic training intervention study but design the post-pandemic replication using a framework analogous to ecological succession. The pandemic is treated as a system-wide psychosocial disturbance. The study would measure multiple outcome dimensions (knowledge, skills, application, trainee well-being) over time to model how the intervention's effectiveness 'recovers' or finds a new trajectory, thereby assessing the intervention's resilience and identifying which components are most or least robust to large-scale societal disruptions.

ideas.title: A Closed-Loop, Metrics-Driven Performance Support System for Multi-State IIS Competency
ideas.description: This research implements and evaluates a dynamic, closed-loop support system designed to achieve sustained competency across multiple states with different IIS platforms. Instead of standardized training, the system establishes key performance indicators (KPIs) for data quality and use within each participating IIS, then links these metrics to an integrated platform delivering just-in-time, context-sensitive learning interventions and workflow aids. The study measures the system's effectiveness in durably improving KPIs compared to traditional training, while also analyzing the adaptation strategies required for different technical and policy environments to identify core principles for scalable, sustainable public health IT support.

ideas.title: Assessing Sustainable Integration: Identifying Organizational Adaptations for Long-term IIS Use in Chain Pharmacies and Health Systems
ideas.description: This study investigates whether IIS training interventions lead to durable practice change by rigorously distinguishing between temporary research effects and sustained integration. Using a stepped-wedge design with a long-term follow-up phase reliant on passive data collection, it measures the persistence of improved IIS documentation for one year post-implementation. The research simultaneously employs mixed methods to map the causal pathways, identifying the essential organizational adaptations within large chains and health systems that correlate with lasting success, thereby providing a blueprint for scalable, sustainable implementation beyond the study context.

ideas.title: Employ mixture modeling to identify latent subgroups
ideas.description: Use finite mixture models to find unobserved subgroups within sexes based on cognitive profiles.
ideas.sourceTitle: Sex Differences in Fluid Reasoning: Manifest and Latent Estimates from the Cognitive Abilities Test

ideas.title: Identifying and Activating Willing Opinion Leaders via Integrated Network-Behavioral Profiling for IIS Adoption
ideas.description: This research system first conducts a social network analysis within a target pharmacy community to identify structurally influential individuals. Concurrently, it administers a behavioral survey to assess each pharmacist's stage of change, attitudes, and perceived capability regarding Immunization Information System (IIS) use. By integrating these two datasets, the system identifies a cohort of 'Activatable Champions'—pharmacists who are both centrally positioned in the peer network and demonstrate high readiness to advocate for IIS. The intervention then provides these selected champions with tailored training and support, transforming latent influence into effective, peer-driven promotion of the specific health technology.

ideas.title: The Adaptive Value of Altruistic Frames: An Empirical Test in Engineering Systems
ideas.description: This research program empirically tests whether engineering projects initiated with explicitly altruistic or prosocial problem definitions exhibit greater long-term sustainability and adaptive capacity than those with strictly instrumental definitions. It treats 'altruistic definition' as an independent variable, measurable through analysis of project charters, requirement documents, and stakeholder communications. Using a mixed-methods approach combining historical case analysis, computational text mining of engineering literature, and simulation modeling, the study seeks to identify causal mechanisms—such as enhanced trust, broader stakeholder inclusion, or resilience to value conflicts—that might explain any observed differential outcomes. The goal is to move beyond normative debate to produce evidence-based guidance for robust system design.

ideas.title: Adopt rapid ethnography from organizational studies
ideas.description: Use intensive, short-term observational methods to capture real-time fidelity dynamics in classrooms.
ideas.sourceTitle: Fidelity of Implementation: Understanding Why and When Programs Work

ideas.title: Apply nudge theory to increase data entry
ideas.description: Incorporate behavioral economics nudges into the IIS interface to prompt and simplify vaccination reporting.
ideas.sourceTitle: Increasing use of immunization information systems for routine vaccinations in independent community pharmacies: A randomized controlled trial

ideas.title: Assess long-term sustainability of training effects using ecological population monitoring methods
ideas.description: This idea adapts longitudinal ecological monitoring methods from population biology to assess the long-term sustainability of IIS training effects. In ecology, researchers track species populations over decades using repeated surveys in established plots to measure persistence, recruitment, and extinction. Adapted to this context, the research would implement a multi-year monitoring framework with regular intervals (e.g., every 6 months for 1-3 years) to track IIS enrollment and participation rates. This approach would distinguish between temporary program engagement and sustained integration, using participant tracking methods analogous to ecological mark-recapture techniques to measure retention rates, participant 'drop-out' dynamics, and potential reactivation patterns beyond the original trial's 3-month horizon.

ideas.title: Explore cost-effectiveness of pharmacist-led MI
ideas.description: Analyze the economic impact and return on investment of the worksite adherence intervention.
ideas.sourceTitle: Pharmacist-led motivational interviewing for diabetes medication adherence in a worksite wellness program.

ideas.title: Multi-stakeholder negotiation support system for co-designing IIS training to optimize systemic data quality
ideas.description: This research applies a negotiation support framework, rather than standard participatory design, to the co-creation of Immunization Information System (IIS) training. It convenes pharmacists, public health officials, EHR vendors, and clinicians in structured sessions to explicitly identify and resolve conflicts between local usability needs and population health data requirements. The resulting training protocol is then iteratively prototyped and validated for its dual impact on user satisfaction and objective IIS data quality metrics, ensuring the solution advances both adoption and systemic interoperability.

ideas.title: Barrier-informed implementation support for IIS integration in pharmacy workflows
ideas.description: This research employs academic detailing visits in a novel, diagnostic role to systematically map the specific technical, economic, and workflow barriers preventing IIS use within diverse pharmacy settings. The qualitative and quantitative data gathered is used to co-design a menu of tailored implementation supports, such as streamlined technical integration protocols, workflow redesign templates, and advocacy briefs for payer reimbursement. The core intervention then tests a collaborative model where pharmacies select and receive support for the barrier-specific solutions most relevant to their context, moving beyond education to active facilitation of system change.

ideas.title: Challenge that sex is a binary, homogeneous category
ideas.description: Analyze data considering sex as a spectrum or interacting with other identities like gender identity.
ideas.sourceTitle: Sex Differences in Fluid Reasoning: Manifest and Latent Estimates from the Cognitive Abilities Test

ideas.title: Challenge that three encounters are the optimal dose
ideas.description: Test a variable or stepped-care number of MI sessions based on individual need.
ideas.sourceTitle: Pharmacist-led motivational interviewing for diabetes medication adherence in a worksite wellness program.

ideas.title: Just-in-Time Immunization: A Cognitive Vaccine Approach to Security Training
ideas.description: This research adapts the just-in-time priming principle from immunology to cybersecurity training. In vaccinology, introducing an antigen contextually primes the immune system for long-term defense. Analogously, we propose developing and testing a system that injects a 'cognitive vaccine'—a 5-minute, ultra-contextual micro-training module—directly into the IIS login or workflow at the precise moment a user's action signals a specific security risk (e.g., before opening a shared file, when connecting to an external network). The solution involves identifying high-risk behavioral triggers as 'antigens' and designing corresponding minimalist training 'doses' to build persistent, threat-specific security awareness, scaling training efficacy by making it dual-purpose: a seamless workflow component that also provides targeted immunization.

ideas.title: Mapping and Mobilizing Context-Dependent Professional Identities via Network Analysis
ideas.description: This research direction applies social network analysis to study how professionals enact different, context-dependent professional identities within their peer communities. Instead of identifying a single, static 'opinion leader,' the method maps which specific professional identity (e.g., clinical expert, efficiency optimizer, patient educator) is most active and influential for each individual within different communication contexts or regarding different topics. This allows for the strategic mobilization of champions by matching the innovation's framing (e.g., a new clinical system) to the specific identity-role in which a professional holds the most sway, thereby increasing adoption through resonant, identity-based influence pathways.

ideas.title: Relax assumption that pharmacists must deliver MI
ideas.description: Evaluate if peer employees or other wellness staff can effectively deliver the adherence intervention.
ideas.sourceTitle: Pharmacist-led motivational interviewing for diabetes medication adherence in a worksite wellness program.

ideas.title: Testing the Durability and Evolution of Nudge-Based Interventions for Sustained IIS Reporting
ideas.description: This study investigates the temporal dynamics of behavioral nudges within an Immunization Information System (IIS) interface. It moves beyond testing a one-time nudge intervention or a one-time training by designing a longitudinal experiment to measure how the effectiveness of specific nudges (e.g., simplification prompts, default options, social norms) decays, sustains, or evolves over months following initial user training. The core research question is whether sustained high-quality data entry requires periodically refreshed or varied 'booster nudges' to combat habituation, or if a well-designed initial nudge suite creates lasting habitual use. This fusion yields a novel framework for the lifecycle management of behavioral interventions in health IT systems.

ideas.title: Compare shock across different transfer pathways
ideas.description: Analyze differences in shock for lateral, reverse, or swirl transfers beyond two-year to four-year moves.
ideas.sourceTitle: STEMing the Shock: Examining Transfer Shock and Its Impact on STEM Major and Enrollment Persistence

ideas.title: A Federated Learning Approach to Multi-State IIS Training
ideas.description: This idea adapts the solution from federated learning in distributed computing to the challenge of scaling an IIS training program across multiple states with different IIS platforms. The analogous solution involves training a central model across decentralized nodes without sharing raw data. Adapted here, we would implement and evaluate the training program by creating a central coordination body that develops a core training curriculum and evaluation metrics. Each participating state then adapts and delivers this core program on its specific IIS platform, collecting local implementation data. Outcomes, adaptations, and lessons learned are anonymized and aggregated centrally. This federated approach allows for the systematic evaluation of the program's effectiveness across diverse systems, enabling iterative refinement of the national training standard based on real-world, distributed feedback, thus achieving scale while accommodating platform and jurisdictional differences.

ideas.title: Disentangling Pandemic and Historical Effects on Intervention Efficacy Using a Multi-Cohort Longitudinal Design
ideas.description: This study moves beyond a simple pre/post-pandemic comparison by recruiting multiple cohorts assessed at the same life stage but exposed to different phases of the pandemic (e.g., a pre-2020 cohort, a 2020-2022 'acute phase' cohort, and a 2023-2025 'post-acute' cohort). All participants undergo the same intervention and are assessed longitudinally. Crucially, the design includes granular measurement of pandemic-related experiences (e.g., disruption severity, loss, remote work adaptation) and broader socio-emotional factors as potential moderators. This allows analysis to determine if changes in training effectiveness are specifically linked to pandemic exposures or to other secular trends, creating a more robust model of how large-scale societal disruptions influence psychological or behavioral interventions.

ideas.title: Scale down to micro-ethnography of lab groups
ideas.description: Conduct intensive ethnographic observation of small student teams to see identity negotiations in real time.
ideas.sourceTitle: Am I an engineer yet? Perceptions of engineering and identity among first year students

ideas.title: Apply community health worker model
ideas.description: Adapt the lay health worker approach for peer support within the worksite setting.
ideas.sourceTitle: Pharmacist-led motivational interviewing for diabetes medication adherence in a worksite wellness program.

ideas.title: Scale down to a micro-intervention via text

ideas.title: An adaptive maintenance framework for IIS participation: Detecting decay and triggering just-in-time support
ideas.description: This study tests a dynamic, adaptive framework for maintaining IIS participation over time, moving beyond a simple one-time vs. ongoing support comparison. The system continuously monitors participation data and administers brief quarterly 'pulse' surveys to identify early signals of participation decay or emerging barriers. When decay is detected for a participant or clinic, the system triggers a tailored, minimal booster intervention—such as a targeted refresher module, access to updated workflow tools, or a peer consultation—designed to address the specific barrier identified. This approach evaluates the efficacy and efficiency of providing support adaptively, only when needed, to sustain long-term participation in a changing operational environment.

ideas.title: Hybrid Seed-and-Scale: Targeting Independents and a Chain Pilot for Demonstrable Population Impact
ideas.description: This approach critiques the binary choice between independent and chain pharmacies by designing a sequential, evidence-generating pathway. It begins by cultivating a pilot network of independent pharmacies to develop and stress-test implementation workflows in a lower-stakes environment. The operational data and proven integration model from this seed phase are then used to secure and execute a targeted pilot with a major regional or national chain, focusing on a contiguous geographic area. This hybrid strategy mitigates chain integration risk with prior evidence while ensuring the final pilot achieves the high patient coverage needed to generate compelling, population-level proof of concept for IIS impact and cost-effectiveness.

ideas.title: Identifying Pharmacies Resistant to IIS Training: An Ecological Niche Modeling Approach
ideas.description: This research adapts the ecological solution of species distribution modeling (SDM) to the public health problem of identifying pharmacies resistant to Immunization Information System (IIS) training. In ecology, SDMs analyze environmental variables (e.g., temperature, precipitation, land cover) to predict where a species will thrive or fail. Analogously, this study will treat pharmacies as 'organisms' within a healthcare 'ecosystem' and model the 'environmental' variables that define a niche resistant to IIS adoption. The analogous solution involves collecting data on pharmacy characteristics (independent, chain, grocery-based), local demographic and epidemiological factors, existing health IT infrastructure, staffing ratios, and perceived regulatory landscapes. By analyzing these factors for pharmacies that have refused or failed training, a predictive model will be built to identify the specific combination of conditions—the 'resistance niche'—that predicts non-participation. This allows public health agencies to proactively tailor intervention strategies (e.g., different support, incentives, or messaging) for pharmacies occupying this high-resistance niche, thereby improving the efficiency and effectiveness of IIS scaling efforts.

ideas.title: Adapting Ecosystem Keystone Species Analysis to Identify Pharmacist Opinion Leaders for IIS Advocacy
ideas.description: This idea adapts the ecological method of identifying keystone species—species with a disproportionately large impact on their ecosystem's structure and stability—to the problem of finding pharmacist opinion leaders for Immunization Information System (IIS) advocacy. In ecology, keystones are identified through network analysis of interactions (e.g., food webs), using metrics like betweenness centrality to find nodes that connect modules and whose removal critically disrupts network cohesion. Adapted to the research context, we would map the professional social network of pharmacists (via surveys, professional membership data, or collaboration records) and apply these ecological network diagnostics. The goal is to move beyond simple popularity (degree centrality) to identify pharmacists who are essential bridges between different pharmacy practice settings or geographic clusters, and thus whose championing of IIS use would most effectively propagate through and stabilize the entire peer network, ensuring robust information flow and adoption.

ideas.title: Adapting Pharmacist IIS Training for Nurse Practitioners Using a 'Train-the-Trainer' Cascade Model
ideas.description: This idea adapts the successful 'train-the-trainer' cascade model from public health scaling to solve the problem of training nurse practitioners (NPs) in retail settings on Immunization Information Systems (IIS). The analogous solution from community health worker programs involves master trainers educating a core group of supervisors, who then disseminate training widely. Adapted here, certified pharmacist experts in IIS would first train a cohort of lead NPs or clinical supervisors from major retail health providers. This core group would then be equipped and authorized to train NPs within their respective organizations, creating a self-sustaining, scalable training network that maintains standardization and leverages existing clinical hierarchies for efficient rollout.
